steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Find which ordered pair (x, y) satisfies both y = 3x + 13 and y = 3x^2 + 13 simultaneously. Substitute each choice into both equations.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '(0, 0)': y = 3(0) + 13 = 13 ≠ 0. ✗
  • Choice B — '(0, 13)': y = 3(0) + 13 = 13 ✓ and y = 3(0)^2 + 13 = 13 ✓. Both equations hold. ✓
  • Choice C — '(26, 91)': y = 3(26) + 13 = 91 ✓ but y = 3(26)^2 + 13 = 2041 ≠ 91. ✗
  • Choice D — '(26, 169)': y = 3(26) + 13 = 91 ≠ 169. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
